title: CI: bud: skip test that requires hello
body:
Emergency PR to get CI working again.

PR #19719 removed the "hello" subdirectory and got merged
without actually running CI. This breaks a buildah test
that relied on that subdirectory.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-notes
None
```

comments.body: @rhatdan @vrothberg @jakecorrenti @mtrmac @Luap99 please cherrypick this if you want to get your PRs to pass CI

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Given the buildah tests also need wouldn't it be easier to revert the change, then fix the buildah test to use a different dir and wait a while before again removing it. 

Because right now this means we need to emergency backport it to all maintenance branches instead of a single revert PR on main?
That would be a lot less stress, yes, but I don't have a sense for why that remove-hello PR happened or how important it is.

This image was moved to https://github.com/containers/PodmanHello, I don't think it is strictly required to get rid of that content in podman repo. Well at least not until we make sure that no tests are broken.
Closing in favor of #19730 to give us breathing room.
